plainEnglish: At Level AAA, live audio-only broadcasts (like a live radio stream) provide a text alternative such as real-time captions or a live text feed. This gives deaf and hard-of-hearing listeners equivalent access to live audio that has no accompanying video.
whyItMatters: Deaf and hard-of-hearing users can't follow a live audio-only broadcast unless it has an accompanying real-time text feed.
quickCheck: Confirm your live audio stream has an accompanying real-time text transcript feed that updates as people speak.
commonMistakes: Offering a transcript only after the broadcast has ended.
Assuming an audio-only live stream is exempt from providing a text alternative.
Streaming live captions into a region that isn't an aria-live area, so screen readers never announce updates.

codeExamples.label: Live audio with a real-time text feed
Live audio with no real-time text
